creator: john@saltant.com
text:
(In reply to Olivier Cochard from comment #4)

Yes, this solves the problem of the frr service failing to start.

I'm still trying to identify the new, best way to grant an otherwise low privilege user access via vtysh.

# pkg install -y frr9
# sysrc frr_enable=YES frr_daemons="mgmtd zebra staticd"
# service frr start

The service is running and the rc script has initialized /usr/local/etc/frr. At this point, I feel like I should be able to grant vtysh like this:

# pw groupmod frrvty -m neirbowj

However, when neirbowj tries to run vtysh, I get:

vtysh_connect(/var/run/frr/mgmtd.vty): stat = Permission denied

The socket itself is g=rwx, so this must be a problem with the directory it's in.

# ls -ld /var/run/frr; ls -l /var/run/frr/mgmtd.vty
drwxr-x---  2 frr frr 14 Oct 26 15:22 /var/run/frr
srwxrwx---  1 frr frrvty 0 Oct 26 15:22 /var/run/frr/mgmtd.vty

If I

# chmod o+rx /var/run/frr

then neirbowj can run vtysh and interact with the services as desired. This also works if I leave /var/run/frr alone and additionally do this:

# pw groupmod frr -m neirbowj

but I'm uncertain what the point is of having the two different groups.

If you prefer to resolve this ticket and move the follow-up discussion to a different venue, please don't hesitate to say so.
